user_prompt: I want a master error reporting function
start files `inc\error_report.h` and `src\error_report.cpp`, and for the header, in addition to just function prototypes, it will have convenience macros so not all calls need to use all the args.
the signature should be `bool error_f(thefly_error_t, bool blocking, int16_t next_view, const char* tag, const char* format_str, ...);`
if the error is fatal, it is blocking even though there's a blocking arg
`next_view` is negative if we want it to be automatic
if it is blocking, ignore the `next_view` arg
if it is blocking or fatal, use `ErrorView`, otherwise it is a `ModalDialog` with the appropriate dismissal view handoff.
Serial port is used directly, forget `DBG_LOG*` calls. These are always active.
The dialog don't show `tag` but the serial port message does. `tag` is allowed to be null.
The dialog don't show time but the serial port message does, use ClockAgent for time if it ready.
All the `show_fatal_error_f` inside `setup()` and `all_init()` should be renamed to `show_boot_error_f` via a macro.
do this up to here, do not modify any other call sites for showing error yet, I will need to review each one by one first
agent_reply: Implemented the first pass of the master error reporter.
- direct `Serial.printf()` reporting with type, optional tag, and ClockAgent time when synced

I moved the old `show_fatal_error_f(...)` implementation into the new error module as a compatibility wrapper, so the unreviewed runtime call sites still compile and still behave in the old blocking/ErrorView style.

Boot-path calls in `setup()` and the splash boot failure in [src/all_init.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp) now use `show_boot_error_f(...)`. I also added `get_view_error()` so `error_report.cpp` can drive the existing `ErrorView`.
Build passed. Note: the build regenerated `src/version.c`, as usual.

user_prompt: ERRTYPE_REMOTE,     // caused by network conditions, disconnections
I see you added macros for me, add another one for this new enum I am highlighting
agent_reply: Added the missing remote convenience macros in [inc/error_report.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/error_report.h):
`ERRTYPE_REMOTE` was already handled in `error_report.cpp` as `"REMOTE"` for serial output.

user_prompt: bool show_error_dialog(const char* text, uint16_t next_view)
remove the redundant `bool show_error_dialog(const char* text, uint16_t next_view)` in `main.cpp`
agent_reply: Removed `bool show_error_dialog(const char* text, uint16_t next_view)` from [src/main.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp).
I also replaced the remaining call sites:
- The invariant-style callback cases now use `error_unexpected_f(...)`.
- Renamed the private helper inside `error_report.cpp` to `show_error_modal(...)`, so `show_error_dialog(` has no remaining hits.
Removed `bool show_error_dialog(const char* text, uint16_t next_view)` from [src/main.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp).
I also replaced the remaining call sites:
- The invariant-style callback cases now use `error_unexpected_f(...)`.
- Renamed the private helper inside `error_report.cpp` to `show_error_modal(...)`, so `show_error_dialog(` has no remaining hits.

user_prompt: the `thefly_error_t` carries a `ERRTYPE_FLAG_BLOCKING` for the same logic of `blocking` now
`ERRTYPE_FLAG_INVISIBLE` causes no GUI being shown, only serial port, I want to start using these calls for web server stuff later
agent_reply: Removed the `bool blocking` parameter from `error_f()` and moved that behavior fully into `thefly_error_t` flags.
- `ERRTYPE_FLAG_INVISIBLE` logs to serial only and skips GUI entirely.
- Fatal errors still block automatically, even without the flag.
- Error category lookup now strips flags first, so `ERRTYPE_REMOTE | ERRTYPE_FLAG_INVISIBLE` still serial-prints as `REMOTE`.
- Added flag helpers/macros in [inc/error_report.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/error_report.h), including `error_invisible_f(...)` and `error_blocking_invisible_f(...)`.
- Updated existing `error_remote_f(...)` / `error_unexpected_f(...)` call sites to the new macro signature.
Removed the `bool blocking` parameter from `error_f()` and moved that behavior fully into `thefly_error_t` flags.
- `ERRTYPE_FLAG_INVISIBLE` logs to serial only and skips GUI entirely.
- Fatal errors still block automatically, even without the flag.
- Error category lookup now strips flags first, so `ERRTYPE_REMOTE | ERRTYPE_FLAG_INVISIBLE` still serial-prints as `REMOTE`.
- Added flag helpers/macros in [inc/error_report.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/error_report.h), including `error_invisible_f(...)` and `error_blocking_invisible_f(...)`.
- Updated existing `error_remote_f(...)` / `error_unexpected_f(...)` call sites to the new macro signature.

user_prompt: `next_view` can also be `THEFLY_ERROR_INVISIBLE` now, and it does the exact same thing as `ERRTYPE_FLAG_INVISIBLE`
agent_reply: `error_f()` now suppresses GUI if either condition is true:
Serial logging still happens either way.

commit_message: code clean up a bit, better error dialog routing

user_prompt: edit `lib\WifiManager\WifiManager.cpp`, I don't like it being a class object, turn it into a single instance namespace module.
there's a lot of code that looks like `(!wifi_manager)` that is pointless, as if it could be missing, allocating it is not a good idea, it can be totally avoided.
so I want any code that currently looks like `wifi_manager->` to look more like `WifiManager::`
the behavior needs to stay the same, that shouldn't be hard, we never had more than one instance of this thing anyways
It would make the recovery-mode Wi-Fi launch much more easy to think about
agent_reply: Done. `WifiManager` is now a single-instance namespace module instead of an allocated class object.

- Converted [WifiManager.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.h) / [WifiManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.cpp) from class state to namespace API + file-scope module state.
- Removed the global `wifi_manager` pointer, heap allocation, `extern` declarations, and pointless `if (!wifi_manager)` branches.
- Updated `ScrollView`, `NtpSync`, web handlers, Wi-Fi views, error recovery AP launch, and the affected tests.
- Preserved the old constructor side effect by calling `WifiManager::clear()` during boot and in standalone AP tests.
All passed. PlatformIO touched `src/version.c` during builds; I left it as-is.
